main.description: List of {mastodonLink} {type} of all {subTitle}. The data comes from {wikidataLink}.
main.dataSource: Formatted data source in JSON format
main.copyList: List of all Mastodon accounts to copy

doings.sharedBy: The following activities were shared by 5 or more researchers:
doings.tip: Tip: You can also filter by activity in the search, or click on an activity in this list.

statistics.title: Statistics for this list
statistics.note: Statistics only include accounts that have published at least one toot within the last year.

export.intro: As of 18.03.2025: If you want to follow all Mastodon accounts in the table or create a list with all accounts, you can import the CSV file in the standard web interface in your settings.
export.warning: If you want to create a list of accounts, you must first follow all accounts. The "Follow Accounts" must be imported first before the "Account List" can be imported.
export.step1: You can find the settings either by clicking the gear icon or simply clicking "Settings".
export.step4: You will now see a form. Under "Type", select "Following" if you want to follow the accounts, or "Lists". In the next step, under "Data", select the downloaded CSV list. Then import the accounts by clicking "Import file".
export.step5: Tip: If you have created a list, you can view the toots from this list in the advanced view (Settings -> Design -> Use advanced web interface) of Mastodon by clicking the "Lists" icon. You can also pin this list as a permanent column. Initially, this column is empty and fills up gradually.

mapConsent.title: Privacy
mapConsent.description: The map software is provided by the external provider {mapboxLink}. The {privacyLink} applies. By consenting to load the maps, your IP address and other data will be shared with the provider.

mapPopup.scrollCtrl: Use Ctrl + Scroll to zoom the map.
mapPopup.scrollCmd: Use ⌘ + Scroll to zoom the map.

score.title: Score Explanation
score.optimized: Your account is excellently optimized! Keep it up!

displayName.title: Display Name
displayName.description: A display name helps others recognize you at first glance. It can differ from your username and contain emojis or your real name.
displayName.tip: Go to {link} and add a display name.

bio.title: Profile Description (Bio)
bio.description: The profile description gives others an insight into your interests, profession, or personality. It is often the first thing people read.
bio.tip: Tell others about yourself in your {link}.

discoverable.title: Discoverable - "Include profile and posts in search algorithms"
discoverable.description: When this option is enabled, your profile appears in your instance's profile directory. This makes it easier for others to discover new interesting accounts.

indexable.title: Indexable - "Include public posts in search results"
indexable.description: Allows your public posts to be found in search. This significantly increases your visibility in the Fediverse.
indexable.tip: Allow indexing of your posts in {link}.

verifiedLinks.title: Verified Links
verifiedLinks.description: Verified links prove that you own a website. You can do this by adding a special link on your website that points back to your Mastodon profile.
verifiedLinks.tip: Go to {link} and add a rel="me" link on your website.

pinnedPosts.title: Pinned Posts
pinnedPosts.description: Pinned posts appear at the top of your profile and are the first thing visitors see. Use them to highlight important information about yourself or your best posts.
pinnedPosts.tip: Pin interesting posts so profile visitors can see them immediately.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.listsDescription: There are different lists with different categories:
faq.mastodonDescription: Mastodon is a decentralized social network based on open standards. It is part of a larger network called the Fediverse. More information about what Mastodon is can be found here: {joinLink}. Further information can also be found in our german {blogLink}.
faq.howCanBenefit: How can universities benefit from decentralized social networks?
faq.fediverse: Universities of all countries into the Fediverse!
faq.fediverseQuote: While much is being discussed about possible engagement of public broadcasters in the Fediverse, the potential of Fediverse instances at universities has hardly been a topic. Yet it is high time for universities to enter the Fediverse. A call to action.
faq.fullArticle: The full article can be found {netzpolitikLink}, where there is also this worth-reading german article: {interviewLink}.
faq.wikidataDescription: Wikidata is a free knowledge base of the Wikimedia Foundation. You can think of it like Wikipedia, but with structured data. More information is available here: {wikidataLink} or in our blog article: {blogLink}.
faq.wikidataDataExplanation: We did not collect the data manually ourselves, but retrieve data from all universities in Germany every day via an interface.
faq.addDataDescription: Anyone can contribute to Wikidata and add data. More information can be found in the article about {dataObjectsLink}. Specifically, you first need to find the right data object. In our case, the {europaUniLink}. There are various statements there. If you already have a Mastodon account and want to change this Mastodon account, you can simply go to the existing statement "Mastodon address" and edit the content. This is sometimes only possible with an account. If there is no statement about Mastodon yet, you need to add a new statement. With the value "Mastodon address" and then the Mastodon account handle. After pressing "Publish", the value is now available. The next time we fetch data, the value will also be displayed.
